name: Present Continuous Tense (-iyor)
summary: The everyday present tense that covers what is happening now and around now, built with -iyor.
explanation:
The most common present tense in spoken Turkish is the -iyor tense. It covers both 'I am doing' and, in conversation, much of what English calls the simple present. To build it, take the verb stem (drop -mek or -mak from the infinitive) and add -iyor plus a personal ending.

The vowel in -iyor follows four-way harmony, so it is one of -ıyor, -iyor, -uyor, or -üyor depending on the last vowel of the stem (yapıyor, geliyor, okuyor, gülüyor). If the stem ends in a vowel, that vowel drops before the suffix: bekle- becomes bekliyor, not bekleiyor.

The personal endings are -um (ben), -sun (sen), nothing (o), -uz (biz), -sunuz (siz), and -lar (onlar). Because the suffix already carries the o of -yor, these endings stay rounded: geliyorum, geliyorsun, geliyor, geliyoruz, geliyorsunuz, geliyorlar.

examples.en: The children are playing in the park.

commonMistakes: Keeping the stem-final vowel before -iyor. It is bekliyor, not bekleiyor.
Using a flat -iyor for every verb instead of harmonising to -ıyor, -uyor, or -üyor.
Forgetting the rounded personal endings, saying geliyorim instead of geliyorum.
Dropping the -yor when adding -lar, saying gelilar instead of geliyorlar.
Treating this as only continuous; in speech it also covers habitual actions like her gün yürüyorum (I walk every day).
tip: Learn geliyorum (I am coming) as a model. Swap the stem and the four endings -um, -sun, -uz, -sunuz follow the same shape every time.
